messages.content: improve: click the Cancel button in the printer settings page or the back arrow of the web page to return to the portal
To navigate back to the portal, you can either click the Cancel button located in the printer settings page or use the back arrow on your web browser.
To export the alarm information in a work sheet

To export the alarm information into a worksheet, follow these steps:

1. Open the alarm management page on your application.
2. Locate the export option or button available on the page. It may be labeled as "Export," "Export to Worksheet," or something similar.
3. Click on the export option/button.
4. A file download dialog box will appear. Choose a suitable location on your computer to save the exported worksheet.
5. Select the desired file format for the worksheet, such as Excel (.xlsx), CSV (.csv), or PDF (.pdf).
6. Click "Save" or "Download" to initiate the export process.
7. Once the export is completed, navigate to the saved location on your computer and open the exported worksheet.

Now you should have the alarm information exported into a worksheet for further analysis or reference.

Alarm Overview provides filtering options based on specific criteria including severity, a specified time period, and alarm IDs.  This enables the network administrator to prioritize the responses and address the more critical issue first.

The Alarm Overview feature offers convenient filtering options that allow the network administrator to prioritize responses by addressing critical issues first. The filtering criteria available includes:

1. Severity: Administrators can filter alarms based on their severity level. This allows them to focus on high-priority alarms that require immediate attention while filtering out less critical ones.

2. Time Period: The filtering capability allows administrators to specify a particular time period to view alarms. Whether it's today's alarms, the past week, or a custom date range, this feature helps narrow down the displayed alarms to a specific timeframe.

3. Alarm IDs: Administrators can filter alarms by their unique IDs. This helps in quickly identifying specific alarms or monitoring the status of specific devices or components.

By utilizing these filtering options, network administrators can easily navigate through the alarm overview and prioritize their responses based on severity, timeframes, and specific alarm IDs. This streamlined approach enhances efficiency in addressing critical issues promptly.

improve:  Alarm Overview provides filtering options based on specific criteria including severity, a specified time period, and alarm IDs.  This enables the network administrator to prioritize the responses and address the more critical issue first.

The Alarm Overview feature offers robust filtering options, empowering network administrators to effectively prioritize and address critical issues promptly. With the ability to filter alarms based on specific criteria, including severity, time period, and alarm IDs, administrators can streamline their response strategies with greater precision.

By using severity as a filter, administrators can focus their attention on high-priority alarms that demand immediate action, ensuring that critical issues are promptly resolved. This allows for a more efficient allocation of resources and minimizes potential disruptions.

Additionally, the specified time period filter enables administrators to narrow down their view to a specific timeframe, such as today, the past week, or a custom range. This aids in quickly identifying trends or recurring issues, enhancing proactive monitoring and preventing potential network problems.

Furthermore, the alarm ID filter allows administrators to target alarms associated with specific devices or components. This facilitates targeted troubleshooting and monitoring, ensuring that the right resources are allocated to address the relevant issues.

By leveraging these advanced filtering options in the Alarm Overview, network administrators gain enhanced control over their response prioritization, enabling them to address critical issues proactively and maintain optimal network performance.
Ticking the check-boxes in front of each severity level allows users to screen and prioritize the alarms based on their severity.

By utilizing the check-boxes located in front of each severity level, users can easily screen and prioritize alarms according to their severity. This functionality empowers users to quickly filter out and focus on alarms that demand immediate attention, ensuring efficient prioritization of tasks and timely resolution of critical issues.
Cleared alarms with start time falling within the selected period of time will be filtered and displayed accordingly.

Cleared alarms falling within the selected time period will be automatically filtered and displayed accordingly. This functionality enables users to review and analyze alarm data specifically within the desired timeframe, facilitating efficient monitoring and assessment of the system's performance or issue resolution.
Alarms with ID added to the filter list will be screened out and displayed.

When adding an alarm ID to the filter list, alarms matching that specific ID will be screened out and displayed accordingly. This feature allows users to easily isolate and focus on alarms associated with a particular ID, enabling streamlined troubleshooting, monitoring, and analysis for specific devices or components.
Users can select the date and time one by one with the on-screen tool or input the exact time in the box directly.

Users have the flexibility to choose their preferred method for selecting the date and time within the application. They can either use the on-screen tool to manually select the date and time one by one or simply input the exact time directly into the provided box. This versatile approach caters to varying user preferences and ensures a seamless experience when specifying the desired date and time for filter criteria.

The search box located above the alarm list supports fuzzy search and provides users with a quick and efficient way to locate specific alarms within the portal using keywords (such as the alarm description, specific problem, etc.).

The search box located above the alarm list provides users with a powerful and efficient way to locate specific alarms within the portal using keywords. It supports fuzzy search, which means that users can input partial or approximate terms to find relevant alarms. The search functionality extends to various attributes, including the alarm description, specific problem, or any other relevant information associated with the alarms. This feature streamlines the process of finding specific alarms quickly, enabling users to efficiently navigate and address specific issues within the portal.
